title: KRM resource stream protocol v1 — event envelope
description: One JSON object per SSE `data:` frame. The normative text is spec/v1.md; this schema is the machine-readable shape of what it says. Heartbeats are SSE comments (': heartbeat'), not events.

krmObject.title: A complete projected Kubernetes object
krmObject.description: NOT a schema — a ConfigMap has `data` and no `spec`; a CRD may define any root field. Carried verbatim, minus exactly what the declared projection removed.

resourceVersion.description: Opaque to consumers. A consumer MAY echo it back on save; it MUST NOT parse, compare or order by it.

target.description: An allowlisted upstream id. Never a raw API-server URL.

namespace.description: Absent => a cluster-scoped resource. NOT 'all namespaces'.

name.description: Present => a single-object scope, framed exactly like a collection.

reset.title: reset — a snapshot cycle begins
reset.description: Consumer: mark every known uid unseen. DO NOT PRUNE YET.

projection.description: What this stream removes from every object. Declared so a consumer can distinguish 'absent upstream' from 'the gateway took it away'.

upsert.title: added / modified — here is this object's current complete state
upsert.description: The only two upsert spellings. A consumer MUST treat them identically and REPLACE the authoritative object for that uid; a deep-merge resurrects fields the server deleted. They stay distinct because a UI legitimately animates an arrival differently from a change.

redactedPaths.description: RFC 6901 JSON Pointers into `object` whose values are masked. REQUIRED — empty array when nothing is redacted. Mandatory precisely so a consumer never infers redaction from a value that merely looks like a placeholder.

deleted.title: deleted — a tombstone with identity
deleted.description: The one event with no complete object; there may not be one. If the gateway cannot recover a trustworthy uid it MUST NOT emit this at all — it begins a new snapshot cycle and lets reset…synced prune.

synced.title: synced — the snapshot cycle is complete
synced.description: Consumer: prune every uid still unseen. Pruning happens HERE and nowhere else — a cycle that never reaches synced must prune nothing.

code.description: A stable machine-readable set: a free-form message alone cannot tell a client whether to reconnect or give up.

terminal.description: If true this MUST be the last event and the gateway closes the connection. A consumer using native EventSource MUST call close() — otherwise it reconnects automatically and hammers a forbidden scope forever.
